ip_outcome_note: No suspected_ip flag on this card.

trivia_bullets.text: Galar Mine (no. 160/192) is a Stadium Trainer card from Rebel Clash (SWSH2), the second Sword & Shield expansion, released May 1, 2020. Rebel Clash's 192-card set introduced dozens of Galar-region Pokemon and supported the Sword & Shield games' narrative arc around Chairman Rose's energy project. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh2-160; WebSearch: Rebel Clash (TCG) release]
sources: WebSearch: Rebel Clash release date, Bulbapedia

trivia_bullets.text: Galar Mine is a real in-game location in Pokemon Sword & Shield, connecting Route 3 and Route 4. It is presented as an active mining operation — the tunnels are lit by crystal formations and staffed by mine workers. The game's narrative places it in the plot surrounding Chairman Rose, who directed a boy to gather Wishing Stars from the mine for his secret energy project (the same energy infrastructure project that drives the game's third-act conflict). [WebSearch: Galar Mine Sword Shield lore, game8.co; Serebii.net Pokearth: Galar Mine]

The Stadium effect raises the Retreat Cost of both Active Pokemon by two Colorless Energy. As a symmetrical cost-inflation effect, Galar Mine is typically used in decks that benefit from opponent immobility — pairing with cards that punish the Active Pokemon or that deal escalating damage over time. The card has no printed flavor text; the Stadium art serves as the sole atmospheric layer. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh2-160 oracle_text (rules)]

Within this corpus, Galar Mine is paired via the Connections section with Pikachu (Burning Shadows 40/147), which carries flavor text describing a plan to "gather many Pikachu and make an electric power plant." Together they form the corpus's primary Pokemon-side labor-extraction pair: Galar Mine as physical extraction-site infrastructure (tunnel, rail tracks, wooden support beams), Pikachu BUS-40 as workforce-aggregation-as-energy-resource. [card frontmatter Connections section; [[labor]] hub; Pikachu BUS-40 flavor text via PokemonTCG.io]
sources: PokemonTCG.io API: BUS-40 Pikachu flavor text
